sourceMovie.title: All About Lily Chou-Chou
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit All About Lily Chou-Chou for the same reason it broke through in 2001: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a crime rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Shunji Iwai keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The United States of Leland
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If All About Lily Chou-Chou clicked for you, The United States of Leland (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and crime, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after All About Lily Chou-Chou?
faq.answer: Try The United States of Leland, Kids Return, A Silent Voice: The Movie—each shares DNA with All About Lily Chou-Chou while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is All About Lily Chou-Chou?
faq.answer: All About Lily Chou-Chou (2001) is best described as Drama, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is All About Lily Chou-Chou worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
